data.content:
### Reset Supabase Local Database

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Resets the local database to a clean state and reapplies all migrations from scratch, which is useful for testing purposes. It supports resetting to a specific migration version and can skip the execution of the `seed.sql` file.

```bash
supabase db reset
supabase db reset --version 20240115120000
supabase db reset --no-seed
```

--------------------------------

### Apply or Revert Supabase Database Migrations Locally (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Applies pending database migrations to the local Supabase instance or reverts previously applied migrations. Users can apply all pending migrations, optionally including seed data, or revert a specified number of the most recent migrations. The command provides output detailing the applied migrations and any encountered errors.

```bash
supabase migration up

supabase migration up --include-all

supabase migration down --last 1

supabase migration down --last 3
```

--------------------------------

### Repair Supabase Migration History

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Fixes the migration history table when it's out of sync with the actual schema state of the database. This allows marking specific migrations as either applied or reverted to correct inconsistencies.

```bash
supabase migration repair 20240101120000 20240102120000 --status applied
supabase migration repair 20240103120000 --status reverted
```

--------------------------------

### Frequently Called SQL Queries from `db-calls` Output

Source: https://github.com/supabase/cli/blob/develop/docs/supabase/inspect/db-calls.md

These SQL queries are examples of statements identified by the `supabase db-calls` command as having a high number of calls. They are extracted from a typical command output table which also provides performance metrics like execution time and I/O time, highlighting areas for potential optimization.

```sql
SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1
```

```sql
SELECT * FROM user_events
```

```sql
INSERT INTO users (email, name) VALUES ($1, $2)
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Supabase Project with CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This command permanently deletes a specified Supabase project along with all its associated data, including databases, functions, and storage. The operation requires a confirmation prompt where the project name must be typed, and this action cannot be undone.

```bash
supabase projects delete zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Supabase Database Migration (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Generates a new empty migration file with a timestamp-based filename in the `supabase/migrations/` directory. This file is then edited manually to define database schema changes, such as creating tables and applying row-level security policies.

```bash
supabase migration new create_users_table

# Creates: supabase/migrations/20250124120000_create_users_table.sql

# Edit the file:
# CREATE TABLE users (
#   id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
#   email text UNIQUE NOT NULL,
#   created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
# );
#
# ALTER TABLE users ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
```

--------------------------------

### Lint Supabase Database Schema

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Checks the local database schema for common issues and typing errors. You can specify a minimum error level and configure the command to fail on specific error severities. The output highlights issues like missing indexes, primary key absence, security policy problems, and type inconsistencies.

```bash
supabase db lint --local
supabase db lint --local --level warning
supabase db lint --local --fail-on error
```

--------------------------------

### Analyze Supabase Database Locks

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Identifies blocking queries and lock waits within a linked database. This command can show queries holding exclusive locks and those that are actively blocking other queries, aiding in database concurrency troubleshooting.

```bash
supabase inspect db locks --linked
supabase inspect db blocking --linked
```

--------------------------------

### Unset Supabase Project Secrets

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Commands to remove one or more secrets from a Supabase project using its reference ID. A confirmation prompt appears before deletion to prevent accidental removal.

```bash
# Unset single secret
supabase secrets unset API_KEY --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

# Unset multiple secrets
supabase secrets unset DATABASE_URL API_KEY STRIPE_KEY --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

# Confirmation prompt appears before deletion
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Supabase Edge Function using CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This command removes a specified Edge Function from the remote Supabase project. By default, it prompts for confirmation before deletion, but users can bypass this prompt using the --yes flag for automated scripts or confident removal.

```bash
supabase functions delete hello-world
```

```bash
supabase functions delete hello-world --yes
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Supabase Database Migrations using Go API

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This Go program demonstrates how to connect to a Supabase database, list local and remote migrations, identify pending migrations, and apply them programmatically. It requires database connection details via `SUPABASE_DB_URL` environment variable and local migration files.

```go
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "os"
    "github.com/supabase/cli/pkg/migration"
    "github.com/supabase/cli/pkg/pgxv5"
)

func main() {
    ctx := context.Background()

    // Connect to database
    dbURL := os.Getenv("SUPABASE_DB_URL")
    conn, err := pgxv5.Connect(ctx, dbURL)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }
    defer conn.Close(ctx)

    // List local migrations
    fsys := os.DirFS(".")
    migrations, err := migration.ListLocalMigrations("supabase/migrations", fsys)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    fmt.Printf("Found %d migrations\n", len(migrations))

    // Get remote migration history
    remoteMigrations, err := migration.ListRemoteMigrations(ctx, conn)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    // Find pending migrations
    pending, err := migration.FindPendingMigrations(migrations, remoteMigrations)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    if len(pending) == 0 {
        fmt.Println("No pending migrations")
        return
    }

    // Apply pending migrations
    fmt.Printf("Applying %d pending migrations...\n", len(pending))
    err = migration.ApplyMigrations(ctx, pending, conn, fsys)
    if err != nil {
        panic(err)
    }

    fmt.Println("Migrations applied successfully")
}
```

--------------------------------

### Dump Supabase Database

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Exports the database schema or data from a linked remote database. This command supports dumping the entire schema, data only, roles only, or using the faster `COPY` format for large datasets, and allows excluding specific tables.

```bash
supabase db dump --linked -f backup.sql
supabase db dump --linked --data-only -f data.sql
supabase db dump --linked --role-only -f roles.sql
supabase db dump --linked --data-only --use-copy -f data_copy.sql
supabase db dump --linked --exclude=auth.* -f schema_no_auth.sql
```

--------------------------------

### Push Supabase Migrations to Remote

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Applies local migrations to the remote database. This command supports dry runs to preview changes and can optionally include roles or seed data. The output provides feedback on each migration's application and any errors encountered.

```bash
supabase db push --dry-run
supabase db push
supabase db push --include-roles
supabase db push --include-seed
```

--------------------------------

### Generate Supabase Schema Diff

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Compares the local database state against migration files or a remote database to generate new migration files. It supports diffing specific schemas and leveraging different diffing tools like Migra or pgAdmin.

```bash
supabase db diff --local -f new_changes
supabase db diff --linked -f sync_remote
supabase db diff --local --schema public,auth -f schema_changes
supabase db diff --use-migra --local -f changes
supabase db diff --use-pgadmin --linked -f remote_diff
```

--------------------------------

### Test Supabase Database with pgTAP

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Runs pgTAP tests against the database, supporting execution of all tests or specific test files, including against a linked database. The command also provides functionality to create new test files from a pgTAP template for easier test setup.

```bash
supabase test db
supabase test db supabase/tests/users_test.sql
supabase test db --linked supabase/tests/*.sql
supabase test new test_users --template pgtap
```

```sql
BEGIN;
SELECT plan(3);

SELECT has_table('public', 'users');
SELECT has_column('public', 'users', 'email');
SELECT col_is_unique('public', 'users', 'email');

SELECT * FROM finish();
ROLLBACK;
```

--------------------------------

### Pull Supabase Remote Schema

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Pulls the current schema from a linked remote database and creates a new migration file. This migration file contains the complete schema, including tables, functions, and policies. You can customize the migration name and review the pulled schema before committing.

```bash
supabase db pull
supabase db pull baseline_schema
supabase db pull remote_snapshot
cat supabase/migrations/*_remote_snapshot.sql
```

--------------------------------

### View Supabase Database Statistics using CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

This section provides commands to inspect various database statistics using the Supabase CLI. It allows users to check cache hit rates, sizes, WAL size, table statistics, index statistics, and vacuum statistics for their linked Supabase project. These commands help in monitoring database performance and health.

```bash
supabase inspect db db-stats --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db table-stats --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db index-stats --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db vacuum-stats --linked
```

--------------------------------

### Link Local Supabase Project to Remote (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Links your local Supabase development environment to a remote project, enabling schema pulling and migration pushing. This requires prior authentication via `supabase login` and can be done interactively or by providing a database password. An option to skip the connection pooler is also available.

```bash
supabase login

supabase link --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng

supabase link --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng --password "your-db-password"

supabase link --project-ref zeoxvqpvpyrxygmmatng --skip-pooler
```

--------------------------------

### List Supabase Database Migrations (CLI)

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Displays a list of all local and remote database migrations along with their application status. This command helps developers track which migrations have been applied and identify any discrepancies between local and linked remote projects.

```bash
supabase migration list --local

supabase migration list --linked
```

--------------------------------

### Monitor Supabase Database Replication using CLI

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

These Supabase CLI commands enable monitoring of database replication slots and traffic patterns. Users can view active replication slots, their plugin type, and lag in bytes, as well as analyze overall traffic profiles for performance and synchronization insights.

```bash
supabase inspect db replication-slots --linked
```

```bash
supabase inspect db traffic-profile --linked
```

--------------------------------

### Squash Supabase Migrations

Source: https://context7.com/supabase/cli/llms.txt

Combines multiple migration files into a single one for a cleaner history, moving original files to a branches directory. This command supports squashing all migrations or only those up to a specific version.

```bash
supabase migration squash
supabase migration squash --version 20240115120000
```
